item.title: Mbappé penalty sends France into World Cup quarter-finals after 1-0 win over Paraguay
item.description: France have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Paraguay in Philadelphia, with Kylian Mbappé’s second-half penalty proving decisive. The World Cup favourites were made to work for their victory as Paraguay produced a disciplined defensive display to frustrate Les Bleus for long periods.
item.content:encoded:
France have booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Paraguay in Philadelphia, with Kylian Mbappé’s second-half penalty proving decisive.

The World Cup favourites were made to work for their victory as Paraguay produced a disciplined defensive display to frustrate Les Bleus for long periods.

The match remained goalless at half-time, with neither side registering a shot on target.

France finally broke the deadlock after substitute Désiré Doué was fouled in the box, with the penalty awarded following a VAR review. Mbappé then converted from the spot to seal the victory.

Paraguay, who had earlier upset Germany on penalties in the Round of 32, defended strongly throughout but were unable to overcome France’s dominance in possession.

France will face Morocco in the World Cup quarter-finals after Morocco beat Canada 3-0 earlier today.

item.title: Morocco beat Canada 3-0 to reach World Cup quarter finals
item.description: Canada's first FIFA World Cup campaign as a tournament co-host has come to an end after a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in Houston. After a goalless first half, Azzedine Ounahi gave Morocco the lead five minutes into the second half.
item.content:encoded:
Canada's first FIFA World Cup campaign as a tournament co-host has come to an end after a 3-0 defeat to Morocco in Houston.

After a goalless first half, Azzedine Ounahi gave Morocco the lead five minutes into the second half.

Ounahi scored his second with eight minutes remaining before Soufiane Rahimi added a third in stoppage time.

Morocco have advanced to the quarter finals where they will face the winner of the Round of 16 clash between France and Paraguay.

The Atlas Lions are making their seventh FIFA World Cup appearance, having first competed at the tournament in 1970.

Their greatest World Cup run came in 2022, when they became the first African team to reach the semi-finals.

Morocco eventually finished fourth at the 2022 FIFA World Cup after historic knockout-stage victories over Spain and Portugal.

Meanwhile, France and Paraguay will play at 9am.

item.title: Ardie Savea urges young people to “shine your light”
item.description: All Blacks captain Ardie Savea has delivered an emotional message of encouragement to young people from underprivileged backgrounds, saying self-belief is key to overcoming life’s challenges. Savea, reflecting on his journey from humble beginnings to captaining the All Blacks, says his rise to the captaincy is proof that potential exists in every individual, regardless of their circumstances.
item.content:encoded:
All Blacks captain Ardie Savea has delivered an emotional message of encouragement to young people from underprivileged backgrounds, saying self-belief is key to overcoming life’s challenges.

Savea, reflecting on his journey from humble beginnings to captaining the All Blacks, says his rise to the captaincy is proof that potential exists in every individual, regardless of their circumstances.

When asked by an RNZ reporter what advice he would give to children growing up with limited resources, Savea says the focus should be on recognising their personal strengths and using them to succeed.

Insert: Savea - Shine your light July 04 TV

The All Blacks will face France at 7.10pm today.

The Flying Fijians play Wales at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other games, Japan will meet Italy at 8.40 tonight, the Wallabies take on Ireland at 10.40 tonight, the Springboks play England at 3.40am tomorrow, while Argentina will meet Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Argentina beat Cape Verde 3-2 after extra time to set up Egypt clash
item.description: Defending champions Argentina have survived a major scare to beat World Cup debutants Cape Verde 3-2 after extra time and reach the Round of 16. A 111th-minute own goal by Diney Borges proved decisive after Cape Verde twice came from behind to level the scores against the three-time world champions.
item.content:encoded:
Defending champions Argentina have survived a major scare to beat World Cup debutants Cape Verde 3-2 after extra time and reach the Round of 16.

A 111th-minute own goal by Diney Borges proved decisive after Cape Verde twice came from behind to level the scores against the three-time world champions.

Lionel Messi opened the scoring in the 29th minute, taking his all-time FIFA World Cup tally to 20 goals.

However, Cape Verde, ranked 64th in the world, refused to give up and Deroy Duarte equalised in the second half to make it 1-all.

The match went into extra time , where Lisandro Martínez restored Argentina’s lead in the 92nd minute.

Cape Verde responded again 11 minutes later through Sidny Lopes Cabral, who scored a stunning curling goal to make it 2-all.

Cabral almost put Cape Verde ahead late in extra time, but Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez produced a crucial save.

Argentina eventually secured the win in the 111th minute when Diney Borges scored an own goal.

Argentina will now face Egypt in the Round of 16 after the Pharaohs beat Australia 4-2 on penalties following a 1-all draw after extra time.

Meanwhile, Colombia will meet Ghana at 1.30pm.

item.title: Flying Fijians’ dangerous attacking game set to test rebuilding Wales
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians will look to unleash their dangerous broken-play and offloading game against a rebuilding Wales side when they kick off their Nations Championship campaign in Cardiff tomorrow. In an unusual twist, Fiji will be the home side when they take on Wales at the Cardiff City Stadium.
item.content:encoded:
The FIJI Water Flying Fijians will look to unleash their dangerous broken-play and offloading game against a rebuilding Wales side when they kick off their Nations Championship campaign in Cardiff tomorrow.

In an unusual twist, Fiji will be the home side when they take on Wales at the Cardiff City Stadium.

Wales are going through a rebuilding phase under coach Steve Tandy after a difficult season, but they will know the Flying Fijians can punish any mistakes, especially from loose kicks and broken play.

Fiji are at their most dangerous when the game opens up, with their ability to move the ball quickly, attack the wider channels and keep play alive through offloads.

Wales are expected to use a disciplined and low-risk kicking game to try to stop Fiji from launching dangerous counterattacks.

However, if Wales kick loosely into open space without a strong and connected chase line, the Flying Fijians could quickly expose them out wide.

Wales are also expected to avoid committing too many defenders around central breakdowns, as this could create space for Fiji on the edges.

Their defensive plan could instead focus on forcing Fiji into tighter areas in the middle of the field, where double tackles could be used to limit the Flying Fijians’ offloading game.

The opening round of the inaugural Nations Championship will also feature several major clashes, with New Zealand facing France, South Africa taking on England, Australia meeting Ireland, Argentina playing Scotland, and Japan hosting Italy.

Every match will count towards the Nations Championship standings as teams begin their push towards the Finals Weekend at Allianz Stadium in London in November.

Fiji has beaten Wales twice with the most recent in Cardiff in November 2024, 24-19.

The Flying Fijians play Wales at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other games, the All Blacks will face France at 7.10pm today, Japan will meet Italy at 8.40 tonight, the Wallabies take on Ireland at 10.40 tonight, the Springboks play England at 3.40am tomorrow, while Argentina will meet Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Egypt beat Australia 4-2 on penalties to reach World Cup last 16
item.description: Egypt have claimed their first-ever win in the FIFA World Cup knockout stage after beating Australia 4-2 on penalties to reach the Round of 16. The match was locked at 1-all after extra time.
item.content:encoded:
Egypt have claimed their first-ever win in the FIFA World Cup knockout stage after beating Australia 4-2 on penalties to reach the Round of 16.

The match was locked at 1-all after extra time.

Emam Ashour gave Egypt the lead in the 13th minute before Australia equalised in the 55th minute through an own goal by Mohamed Hany.

Neither team was able to score again, sending the match to a penalty shootout.

Egypt kept their composure to win the shootout 4-2, with Mohamed Salah among the scorers.

An emotional Salah celebrated the victory with his teammates, although there are concerns over his fitness ahead of the next round.

Speaking after the win, Salah says it is history and he had told the players before the game that this was the biggest stage they could play on.

Egypt will face either Argentina or Cape Verde in the Round of 16.

Meanwhile, Cape Verde will face a stern test against Argentina in the next hour, while Colombia will meet Ghana at 1.30pm.

item.title: Ba to celebrate first Fiji FACT title in 16 years with fans at Govind Park
item.description: Ba football fans will have the opportunity to celebrate the club's first BiC Fiji FACT title in 16 years when the Men-in-Black present the trophy after todays's Extra Premier League clash against Nadi at Govind Park. Fresh from ending a 16-year wait for the prestigious title, Ba will present the BiC Fiji FACT trophy to home supporters following the final whistle.
item.content:encoded:
Ba football fans will have the opportunity to celebrate the club's first BiC Fiji FACT title in 16 years when the Men-in-Black present the trophy after todays's Extra Premier League clash against Nadi at Govind Park.

Fresh from ending a 16-year wait for the prestigious title, Ba will present the BiC Fiji FACT trophy to home supporters following the final whistle.

Ba Football Association President Praneel Dayal says the celebration is dedicated to the loyal supporters who stood by the club through the highs and lows.

Dayal says the trophy belongs to every Ba supporter whose unwavering belief in the team has been the club's greatest strength, and Ba wants to celebrate the achievement with them.

He has urged fans to wear their black colours, get behind the team against Nadi and remain at Govind Park after the match to celebrate the historic triumph together.

management, coaching staff, players, volunteers and supporters for their commitment throughout the campaign.

Ba defeated Navua in last Sunday's BiC Fiji FACT final at Subrail Park in Labasa to claim the title for the first time since 2010.

The Extra Premier League match between Ba and Nadi will kick off at 3pm.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Nasinu FC will face Tailevu Naitasiri at 12pm, while Labasa will take on Nadroga at 2pm at Ratu Cakobau Park.

Rewa will play Lautoka at 1pm at Churchill Park while Navua will host Suva at 3pm.

item.description: Rooster Chicken Ba Women FC's hopes of reaching the semi-finals of the OFC Women's Champions League came to an end after a thrilli

item.title: Ba Women lead Tafea FC 3-2 at halftime in crucial OFC group clash
item.description: Ba Women FC are leading Tafea FC of Vanuatu 3-2 at halftime in their crucial Group A clash at the OFC Women’s Champions League 202

item.title: Flying Fijians ready for historic Nations Championship opener tomorrow at 1.10am
item.description: Tomorrow, the FIJI Water Flying Fijians will begin a new chapter in their rugby history when they take on Wales in their opening N

item.title: Ba Women face crucial Tafea clash at 5pm today
item.description: Ba Women FC face a crucial final Group A match against unbeaten leaders Tafea FC of Vanuatu today, with their OFC Women's Champion

item.title: Switzerland advance to Round of 16 after 2-0 win over Algeria
item.description: Switzerland have booked their place in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Algeria 2-0 in their Round of 32 clas

item.title: Flying Fijians complete preparations ahead of Wales Test
item.description: Preparations in camp have been completed by the FIJI Water Flying Fijians, with the squad now ready to take on Wales this Sunday.T

item.title: Goodman Fielder announced as Netball Fiji's new platinum sponsor
item.description: Netball Fiji has received a major boost after Goodman Fielder was announced as its new platinum sponsor to help develop the sport
